name: lgrepSemanticCacheLifecycle
title: LgrepSemanticCacheLifecycle
purpose: Capability: LgrepSemanticCacheLifecycle — defines the invariants and operator-facing surfaces that keep the semantic cache directory (LanceDB chunks + metadata) consistent over time, including orphan detection, manual pruning (CLI + MCP), and lifecycle safety guards.

requirements.title: Cache directory carries reverse-mapping metadata
requirements.body: When a `ChunkStore` is constructed with an explicit `project_path`, it MUST write a `project_meta.json` file next to the LanceDB cache on disk. The metadata MUST at minimum record the resolved absolute project path. The write MUST be atomic (write-to-tmp then rename) to avoid partial reads. When `project_path` is omitted, the constructor MUST NOT write meta (writing the cache hash dir as the project path corrupts orphan detection).

scenarios.title: Explicit project_path writes meta
given: A fresh cache directory
A ChunkStore constructed with a resolved project_path
then: `project_meta.json` exists in the cache directory
The file's `project_path` field equals the resolved project path
The write is atomic (no partial file observable by concurrent readers)

scenarios.title: Omitted project_path skips meta
given: A fresh cache directory
A ChunkStore constructed without a project_path argument
then: No `project_meta.json` is written
The cache hash dir is never recorded as a project_path

scenarios.title: Corruption recovery rewrites meta
given: A cache directory whose existing LanceDB connection fails and must be re-created
A ChunkStore reconstructed with project_path after the corruption recovery
then: `project_meta.json` is present after recovery
Its contents reflect the supplied project_path

requirements.title: Orphan detection classifies four stable reasons
requirements.body: `find_orphans` MUST classify candidate cache directories using exactly four stable orphan reasons: `missing_meta`, `unreadable_meta`, `missing_chunks_lance`, and `project_path_enoent`. Candidates whose name does not match the semantic cache hash shape (`^[0-9a-f]{12}$`) MUST be skipped outright. Symlinked candidates MUST be refused during scan to prevent any downstream operation from following them. Transient filesystem errors on the project_path side (e.g., `PermissionError` on an unmounted drive) MUST preserve the cache rather than mark it orphan.

scenarios.title: Missing meta is detected
given: A cache-shaped directory with chunks.lance/ but no project_meta.json
then: The directory is reported with reason `missing_meta`

scenarios.title: Unreadable meta is distinguished from missing
given: A cache-shaped directory whose project_meta.json exists but is not valid JSON
then: The directory is reported with reason `unreadable_meta`
The reported project_path is null

scenarios.title: Missing chunks.lance is detected
given: A cache-shaped directory with a valid project_meta.json but no chunks.lance/ subdir
then: The directory is reported with reason `missing_chunks_lance`

scenarios.title: Project-path ENOENT is detected
given: A cache-shaped directory whose project_meta.json points at a project path that no longer exists
then: The directory is reported with reason `project_path_enoent`

scenarios.title: Non-hash-shaped children are skipped
given: A cache root containing a child named `symbols/` and a child named `unrelated/`
then: Neither child is reported as an orphan
dirs_examined excludes both because they do not match the cache hash shape

scenarios.title: Symlinked candidates are refused
given: A cache-shaped name that is a symlink to an unrelated directory
then: The symlink is not reported as an orphan
Its target is not visited or deleted

scenarios.title: Transient PermissionError preserves cache
given: A cache whose project_path raises PermissionError when checked
then: The cache is NOT reported as project_path_enoent
No orphan entry is emitted for it

scenarios.title: Active in-memory projects are skipped
given: A cache-shaped directory whose project path is present in the server's active_set
then: The cache is listed under `skipped_active`
It never appears in `orphans`

requirements.title: Prune is dry-run by default and projects reclaim
requirements.body: Both the `prune_orphans` core function and every operator-facing surface (CLI, MCP) MUST default to `dry_run=True`. A dry-run response MUST include the projected reclaim (`reclaimed_bytes = sum of orphan bytes`) so operators can preview savings without mutating disk. `--execute` and `--dry-run` MUST be mutually exclusive on the CLI to avoid last-flag-wins surprises.

scenarios.title: Dry-run preserves disk state
given: An orphan cache directory on disk
scenarios.when: prune_orphans runs with dry_run=True (or the CLI default)
then: The orphan directory still exists after the call
deleted_dirs is 0
reclaimed_bytes equals the sum of orphan[].bytes

scenarios.title: CLI rejects simultaneous --execute and --dry-run
given: A CLI invocation passing both --execute and --dry-run
then: The command exits with a non-zero status
stderr explains that --execute and --dry-run are mutually exclusive
prune_orphans is not invoked

requirements.title: Destructive prune applies path-confinement, TOCTOU, and grace guards
requirements.body: When `dry_run=False`, `prune_orphans` MUST enforce three guards before any `shutil.rmtree` call: (1) path-confinement — the resolved orphan path must be strictly under the resolved cache root; (2) TOCTOU — the orphan path must not be a symlink at delete time; (3) grace — orphan reasons that can be produced by a mid-write indexer (`unreadable_meta`, `missing_chunks_lance`) MUST NOT be reported for caches modified within a grace window (default 3600 seconds, overridable via `LGREP_PRUNE_MIN_AGE_S`; `0` disables). Unambiguous reasons (`missing_meta`, `project_path_enoent`) bypass the grace check. Per-entry failures (rmtree raises, confinement or symlink refusal) MUST be recorded in `failures[]` and MUST NOT abort the batch.

scenarios.title: Paths outside the cache root are refused
given: A tampered orphans list containing a path outside the resolved cache root
then: The out-of-root target is not deleted
failures[] records the refusal with an 'outside cache root' error
In-scope orphans are still processed

scenarios.title: Symlink orphans are refused at delete time
given: A hash-shaped cache entry that is a symlink swapped in after scan
then: shutil.rmtree is not called on the symlink target
The symlink may be recorded in failures[] with a 'symlink' error
The decoy target directory remains intact

scenarios.title: Recently modified ambiguous orphans are held back
given: A cache with unreadable_meta whose mtime is within the grace window
LGREP_PRUNE_MIN_AGE_S > 0
then: The cache is NOT reported as an orphan
No deletion attempt is made

scenarios.title: Unambiguous reasons bypass grace
given: A fresh cache with missing_meta
then: The cache IS reported as missing_meta and eligible for prune

scenarios.title: Per-entry rmtree failures do not abort the batch
given: Two orphan cache directories, where shutil.rmtree is patched to fail on the first
then: The first directory still exists and appears in failures[]
The second directory is deleted
deleted_dirs equals 1

requirements.title: MCP prune_orphans coerces dry_run on non-stdio transports
requirements.body: The MCP `prune_orphans` tool MUST coerce `dry_run=True` whenever the server transport is not `stdio` (or an equivalent local transport). The transport kind MUST be carried on the application context (set from `LGREP_TRANSPORT` at server startup). Unknown or absent transport MUST be treated as non-local. The tool's description MUST tell callers to use the CLI for destructive prunes on shared deployments.

scenarios.title: stdio transport allows caller-chosen dry_run
given: An LgrepContext with transport=stdio
An MCP caller requesting dry_run=False
then: The effective dry_run matches the caller's request

scenarios.title: HTTP transport forces dry_run=True
given: An LgrepContext with transport=streamable-http
An MCP caller requesting dry_run=False
then: The response's dry_run field is True
deleted_dirs is 0
The on-disk orphan still exists

requirements.title: Prune responses use typed dicts aligned with the MCP response pattern
requirements.body: The MCP `prune_orphans` tool MUST return a structured dict matching the `PruneOrphansResult` TypedDict, containing `dry_run`, `dirs_examined`, `orphans`, `skipped_active`, `deleted_dirs`, `reclaimed_bytes`, `failures`, and `_meta`. Orphan entries MUST conform to `PruneOrphanEntry` (`path`, `reason`, `bytes`, `project_path`). Failure entries MUST conform to `PruneFailureEntry` (`path`, `error`). This aligns the new MCP surface with the repo-wide typed-response convention.

scenarios.title: MCP dry-run response carries every required key
given: An MCP prune_orphans call in dry-run mode
then: The response is a dict (not a JSON string)
The keys {dry_run, dirs_examined, orphans, skipped_active, deleted_dirs, reclaimed_bytes, failures, _meta} are all present
